title: CredentialManager executeGetCredential enforceCallingPackage bypass for GMS + getCandidateCredentials autofill-only
pattern: Missing permission check on alternative code path
notes: DEVICE CONFIRMED. Multiple findings: (1) executeGetCredential('com.google.android.gms') from shell uid 2000 — passes enforceCallingPackage (RemoteException not SecurityException), while chrome/settings/whatsapp are properly rejected. GMS has special path allowing callingPackage spoof. (2) getCandidateCredentials blocked by separate 'credential autofill package' check — not exploitable from shell. (3) getCredentialProviderServicesForTesting() zero-perm — returns PasswordAndPasskeyService on production device. (4) clearCredentialState('com.android.shell') succeeds — shell can clear its own credential state. (5) getCredentialProviderServices completely zero-perm with cross-user (V-189 confirmed separately). Original code audit findings about missing enforceCallingPackage in getCandidateCredentials are partially confirmed — the autofill check provides a different gate, but the GMS bypass in executeGetCredential is a new finding.
